vocab.meaning: một bữa tiệc thị giác (thứ gì đó rất đẹp để ngắm nhìn)
vocab.example: The new exhibition at the national gallery is a total feast for the eyes, featuring vibrant colors and intricate textures.

vocab.meaning: mở rộng tầm hiểu biết/nhãn quan
vocab.example: I believe that visiting art galleries helps to broaden my horizons by exposing me to different cultures and perspectives.

vocab.meaning: điều gì đó gợi suy nghĩ, đáng để suy ngẫm
vocab.example: Some of the abstract installations were quite bizarre, but they certainly provided plenty of food for thought.
You don't need to be an expert to appreciate art; once you spend enough time looking at a piece, you start to get the picture of what the artist intended.

vocab.meaning: gây ấn tượng cực mạnh, khiến ai đó sửng sốt
vocab.example: The scale of the sculptures in the main hall absolutely blew my mind; I've never seen anything so grand.

vocab.meaning: mở đường cho, tạo điều kiện cho (phong cách/xu hướng mới)
vocab.example: I enjoy learning about how early impressionist painters paved the way for modern abstract art.

vocab.meaning: một luồng gió mới (thứ gì đó mới mẻ, thú vị)
vocab.example: After a week of staring at spreadsheets, spending a quiet afternoon in a gallery is a real breath of fresh air.

vocab.meaning: kỳ quặc, độc lạ, không chính thống
vocab.example: I'm particularly fond of independent galleries because they often showcase art that is a bit off the wall and unconventional.

vocab.meaning: mải mê suy nghĩ, trầm ngâm
vocab.example: I love the silence of galleries where I can stand in front of a landscape and get completely lost in thought.

vocab.word: beauty is in the eye of the beholder
vocab.ipa: /ˈbjuː.ti ɪz ɪn ðə aɪ əv ðə bɪˈhəʊl.dər/
vocab.meaning: vẻ đẹp nằm trong mắt kẻ si tình (tùy vào cách nhìn của mỗi người)
vocab.example: My friend hated the modern art exhibit, but I found it fascinating—I guess beauty is in the eye of the beholder.
